title: nyc-commons — Identifiers
description: The cross-agency join keys of NYC government. These are the identifiers that recur across domains and, if honored consistently, let a Buildings record join a Finance record join a Housing record join a 311 request. BBL and BIN are the property spine (present in 52 of 67 domains via the linkage analysis); the agency-specific keys (gisPropNum, DBN, matterId) are the primary keys of their owning domain but travel into others. Domains should `$ref` these rather than re-declaring the format.

BBL.description: Borough-Block-Lot — the 10-digit citywide tax-lot key (1 borough digit + 5 block + 4 lot). DCP's PLUTO/MapPLUTO is authoritative. The primary geographic join key across NYC datasets.

BIN.description: Building Identification Number — 7-digit building key (leading digit = borocode). Assigned by DCP; joins buildings to their lot (BBL).

GISPropNum.description: NYC Parks property number (`gispropnum`, e.g. 'M010') — the authoritative key for a park, playground, or greenspace. Parks' equivalent of a BBL.

DBN.description: District-Borough-Number — the NYC public-school identifier (e.g. '01M448'): 2-digit district + borough letter + 3-digit school number. DOE's primary key.

BoroBlockLot.description: BBL decomposed into its three components, as some agencies store them separately.

CrossAgencyKey.description: A generic typed reference to another agency's primary key carried on a record — the mechanism by which the city becomes one addressable graph. e.g. { keyType: 'BBL', value: '1000160001' } or { keyType: 'DBN', value: '01M448' }.

owningDomain.description: The domain slug that is authoritative for this key (e.g. 'dcp', 'nycgovparks.org', 'schools.nyc.gov').
